claim_status_justification: Both img_1 and img_2 show the laptop screen at high quality (5/5) with the screen area clearly visible and fully inspectable, yet neither image reveals any cracks, fractures, or screen damage of any kind, directly contradicting the claim of a cracked screen.

user_claim: Customer: My black car door is dented. | Support: Is the damaged door on the black car? | Customer: Yes, black car door. | Support: Are other vehicle photos only context? | Customer: I uploaded what I had, but the claim is for my black car door.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one usable image shows the claimed car part clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition. Standard (REQ_CAR_BODY_PANEL): The claimed car panel or bumper should be visible from an angle where surface marks or deformation can be assessed.
claim_status_justification: img_2 clearly shows a large dent with crumpled and deformed sheet metal across the upper portion of the car door, along with a secondary dent near the lower door area, directly supporting the claimed dented door. While img_3 shows no damage and img_1 is inconclusive, the high-quality evidence in img_2 is sufficient to support the claim, though the risk flags (claim_mismatch, user_history_risk) warrant manual review.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop keyboard is damaged. | Support: What kind of damage? | Customer: A key is missing or broken. | Support: Is it definitely the laptop keyboard? | Customer: Yes, laptop keyboard damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one usable image shows the claimed laptop part clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition. Standard (REQ_LAPTOP_SCREEN_KEYBOARD_TRACKPAD): The claimed laptop screen, keyboard, or trackpad area should be visible clearly enough to inspect cracks, stains, missing keys, or surface damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows multiple detached keycaps with exposed switch mechanisms supporting the claim, but img_2 shows the laptop with no visible keyboard damage, and multiple risk flags (wrong_object, non_original_image, claim_mismatch) raise serious doubt about whether img_1 depicts the same device being claimed; the conflicting evidence and flags prevent a definitive determination.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop slipped while I was packing my bag. | Support: What part should we review? | Customer: One corner of the laptop is dented now. | Support: Is this about the body, screen, or keyboard? | Customer: Body only, specifically the laptop corner dent.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one usable image shows the claimed laptop part clearly enough to inspect the claimed condition. Standard (REQ_LAPTOP_BODY_HINGE_PORT): The claimed laptop hinge, lid, corner, body, base, or port should be visible with enough context to identify the relevant laptop part.
claim_status_justification: img_2 clearly shows a dent or impact deformation on the upper-left corner of the laptop lid, with a visible depression/buckle in the brushed metal surface consistent with the claimed dented corner. img_1 shows no damage on a different view, but the positive evidence in img_2 is sufficient to support the claim, though risk flags including claim_mismatch and non_original_image warrant manual review.
